market_regime.label: GEOPOLITICAL_RISK_ELEVATED_CEASEFIRE_FRAYING_HORMUZ_ACTIVE
market_regime.key_risk: Brent surge overnight +7.5% to $105.07 on Israel strike threats and Hormuz shipping disruption. US-Iran ceasefire fraying. Weekend binary event risk elevated — Israel-Iran escalation could push Brent to $110+ on Monday gap.
market_regime.dominant_risk: Weekend escalation risk — Israel threats of strikes against Iran, Hormuz shipping blocked, FII selling 4th day. Nifty opened gap-down -0.24%, below psychological 24,150. 24,000 pivotal support.
market_regime.crude_note: Brent surged $7.31 overnight (+7.48%) to $105.07 on Israel strike threats vs Iran + persistent Hormuz blockade. Back firmly in RISK_ELEVATED zone. Break above $108 = escalation signal; need <$95 for regime relief.
market_regime.regime_note: Regime shifted back to RISK_ELEVATED / HORMUZ_ACTIVE on overnight Brent surge. Friday positioning decidedly risk-off. Watch for weekend catalyst: Israel action, Iran response, US diplomatic moves.

session_direction.key_assumption: Brent surge to $105-106 overnight negates EOD 'oversold bounce' thesis. Regime back to RISK_ELEVATED with Israel-Iran escalation concerns. Weekend binary event risk (Israel attacks threatened, Hormuz shipping blocked) drives Friday risk-off positioning — typical 'sell into weekend' flow. IT sector continues under pressure (Nifty IT -4.95% post-HCLTech guidance shock), no fresh catalyst to reverse. FII selling extended to 4th consecutive day (~₹3,254 cr). Key support 24,000 (psychological + recent swing); if breaks, 23,900 next. Upside capped at 24,200-24,220. Defense (ASTRA MICRO, MAZDOCK) likely to firm on geopolitical premium. OMC/Aviation (MRPL, INDIGO) under pressure from Brent surge. Mean-reversion bounce possible intraday but unlikely to hold into close.

scenarios.name: BEARISH_BASE (55%)
scenarios.description: Nifty drifts lower through session, BankNifty leads down, closes 23,950-24,100. IT weak, defense strong. Friday risk-off into weekend confirmed.

scenarios.name: MID_SESSION_BOUNCE_FADE (30%)
scenarios.description: Morning weakness bottoms 24,050-24,080, mid-session short-cover bounce to 24,180-24,200, fades in last hour on weekend positioning. Closes flat to -0.3%.

scenarios.name: ESCALATION_SPIRAL (15%)
scenarios.description: Fresh Israel-Iran headline intraday, Brent to $110+, Nifty breaks 24,000 decisively, closes 23,850-23,950. Defense ripper, IT decimated.

ceasefire_binary_matrix.key_signal: Overnight shift: Israel threatening attacks, Brent surged +7.5% to $105+. Ceasefire fraying rapidly. Next 72 hours binary — fresh escalation flips to collapse.
ceasefire_binary_matrix.ceasefire_status_note: Ceasefire technically extended Apr 22 AM by Trump. Iran seized 2 ships in Hormuz Apr 22 PM after extension. Intraday Apr 23 Brent crash suggests partial easing not reflected in headlines yet.

-1.43% today, worst performer. Mean-reversion candidate Friday if oversold bounce materializes. Watch if it leads or lags the bounce.
Brent at $97.76 (down from $101.57 overnight) supports OMC margins. P017 target 200-210 by May 8 still live. Monitor 185-190 for entry signal.
Aviation relief if Brent holds <$100 into weekend. Monitor for breakout from recent consolidation.
P025 closed ABOVE_RANGE at 1129.75. P030 thesis (1010-1060 on ceasefire extension) is live. Watch for consolidation/profit-booking; +101% portfolio gain argues for trim on strength.

Most important single macro read. $97.76 close. Break below $95 = regime shift to TALKS_REVIVING; back above $100 = collapse confirmation. Overnight binary.
P001 check Apr 25 (target <260). Still at ~294. Will score WRONG.

prompt_for_morning_brief: MORNING BRIEF 2026-04-24: (1) Score opening bias — forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP (+0.15%, GIFT est 24,210). Compare against ACTUAL NSE open. (2) Scan for overnight Hormuz/Iran headlines — critical binary overnight. Brent $97.76 close is cooling — did it hold <$100 or re-spike? (3) Today 2026-04-23 session result: Nifty -0.84% to 24,173.05 (second bearish day). Forecast was VOLATILE_SIDEWAYS_SLIGHT_BEARISH (-0.2%) — graded PARTIAL_CORRECT (in range, direction right, magnitude 4.2x off). (4) M013 confirmed 5th time on Infosys — IT sells the news regardless of earnings beat. (5) Oversold bounce base case for Friday; weekend positioning skews mildly positive; BankNifty -1.43% leaves room for mean-reversion.

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP (+0.15%, GIFT est 24,210). Actual gap -0.24% (24,115.55 vs prev close 24,173.05) — GAP_DOWN direction opposite of forecast. Overnight regime change: Brent surged +7-8% from $97.76 close to $105-106 (Israel threatens Iran attacks, Hormuz shipping remains blocked, US-Iran ceasefire fraying). The constructive 'oversold bounce' thesis collapsed on Brent surge. FII selling extended to 4th session (~₹3,254 cr). Graded WRONG — direction opposite, magnitude off by 0.4pts. session_direction.key_assumption: Technical oversold bounce is base case after two-day -1.65% selloff. Brent at $97.76 is constructive if it holds below $100 overnight.
